title: Full Moon
content:
One night as Dick lay half asleep,
        Into his drowsy eyes
    A great still light begins to creep
        From out the silent skies.
    It was lovely moon's, for when
        He raised his dreamy head,
    Her surge of silver filled the pane
        And streamed across his bed.
    So, for a while, each gazed at each -
        Dick and the solemn moon -
    Till, climbing slowly on her way,
        She vanished, and was gone.
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
